<!-- hal-version: 0.2.194 -->

# hal — Agent File Management

This repository is managed by hal. hal manages agent instruction files,
docker-compose projects, git worktrees, and more across all your repositories.

This file and all CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md files are auto-generated.
Do NOT edit them — they will be overwritten on `hal agent install`.
Put project-specific instructions in `PROJECT.md` instead.

## Agent File Convention

| File | Purpose | Edited by |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `HAL.md` | hal-managed instructions | hal (auto-generated) |
| `PROJECT.md` | Project-specific instructions | You |
| `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` / `GEMINI.md` | Pointers that tell AI agents to read HAL.md + PROJECT.md | hal (auto-generated) |

This pattern repeats in domain subdirectories (e.g., `docker-compose/`).
User-wide domain settings live in `~/.config/hal/<domain>/USER.md`.

## Repository Structure

### docker-compose/
Docker-compose project management via `hal dc`.
- `docker-compose/HAL.md` — hal docker-compose rules and conventions
- `docker-compose/PROJECT.md` — repo-specific docker-compose notes
- `~/.config/hal/docker-compose/USER.md` — personal settings (port ranges, domains, infrastructure)

### .hal/codebase/
Deterministic codebase analysis from 8+ real analysis tools (scc, ast-grep, jscpd, trivy, madge, etc.).
Contains: LANGUAGES.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, SYMBOLS.md, STRUCTURE.md, DEPS-GRAPH.md, DUPLICATION.md, QUALITY.md, SECURITY.md.
Run `hal codebase map` to generate. Run `hal codebase install-tools` to install analyzers.
Read specific files when relevant to your task (not all at once).

### .hal/
hal metadata directory (worktree config, aliases, backups).

## Environment Variables (.env)

Each repo uses **per-environment `.env` files at the repo root**:

- `.env.local-development` — variables for local docker-compose projects
- `.env.remote-production` — variables for production deployments
- `.env` — legacy fallback; used if no env-specific file exists

Generate from templates: `hal env generate <env-name>` renders `.env.template.<env-name>` into `.env.<env-name>`.

**NEVER edit `.env.*` files directly** — they are auto-generated. To change environment variables, edit the `.env.template.*` files instead. `hal dc start` regenerates automatically when templates change.

In worktrees, `.env.template.worktree` is rendered to `.env.local-development` (with worktree-specific port prefixes).

## Git Conventions

### Commit Messages

- First line: max 60 characters, imperative mood ("add", "fix", "update")
- **On main branch**: `<verb>(<domain>|<sub>|<action>): <msg> (<version>)`
- **In worktrees**: `<verb>(<domain>|<sub>|<action>): <msg>` (no version suffix)
- For project-wide changes: `chore(project): <msg>`

### Worktree Workflow (inside `.worktrees/` or `.hal/git/worktree/`)

1. `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` (or equivalent linter)
2. `cargo fmt --all --check` (or equivalent formatter)
3. `cargo test --workspace` (or equivalent test runner)
4. Build must succeed
5. Commit with message format (no version suffix)
6. `git push`

Do NOT bump versions, update changelogs, or build releases in worktrees.

### Main Branch Workflow

1. Bump patch version
2. Update CHANGELOG.md
3. Run linter, formatter, tests
4. Build release
5. Commit with versioned message, push immediately

### Detecting Workflow

If current directory contains `/.hal/git/worktree/` or `.worktrees/`, use worktree workflow. Otherwise, main branch workflow.

## Docker Compose Deployment

### Local
```sh
hal dc start <project>              # start local project (auto-regenerates env)
hal dc stop <project>               # stop
hal dc reset <project>              # destroy + recreate
hal dc list                         # list all projects with status
hal dc doctor                       # validate all projects
```

### Remote (production)
```sh
hal dc start -e rp <project>        # auto-push files + start on remote
hal dc stop -e rp <project>         # stop on remote
hal dc reset -e rp <project>        # auto-push + destroy + recreate on remote
hal dc log -e rp <project> --follow # stream remote logs
```

Remote commands automatically:
1. Regenerate `.env.<env>` from template (if stale)
2. rsync compose files + env file to `/opt/hal/{repo}/{project}/` on the server
3. Execute docker compose via SSH

## hal CLI Quick Reference

Run `hal --help` for all commands. Key domains:
- `hal dc` — docker-compose project management
- `hal git worktree` — git worktree management
- `hal env generate` — generate .env files from templates
- `hal agent install` — install/update agent files across all repos
- `hal agent doctor` — validate agent file conventions
- `hal codebase map` — deterministic codebase analysis to .hal/codebase/
